revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[arz:هيرانو جينجا]][[de:Hirano-Schrein]][[en:Hirano Shrine]][[es:Santuario Hirano]][[fr:Hirano-jinja]][[ja:平野神社]][[ru:Хирано-дзиндзя]][[zh:平野神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Hirano-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto) honden 3 & 4.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q1154970|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.03261|N|135.73194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qid=Q10444949|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata}}; {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|qid=Q215320|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}}|architecture_style={{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2024}} {{infobox shinto shrine | image = Hirano Shrine Torii Gate off Nishiōji Street.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | caption = Hirano shrine torii gate | deity = Imaki-no-kami (今木神)
Kudo-no-kami (久度神)
Furuaki-no-kami (古開神)
[[Himegami|Hime-no-kami]] (比売神) | consecration_year = 794 | architecture_style = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] }} The {{Nihongo|'''Hirano Shrine'''|平野神社|Hirano-jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, Japan. This shrine is known and popular for its gardens and many trees. ==History== The shrine was established in the year 794 by [[:en:Emperor Kammu|Emperor Kammu]] when the capital was transferred to {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}} from [[Nagaoka-kyō]]. From the earliest years, the shrine has been often visited by members of the Imperial family. In earlier centuries, the shrine also has connected a special relationship with both the [[Minamoto clan|Genji]] and the [[Taira clan|Heiji]]. It was the saved place from nature powered cases. And spirit Genji and Heiji sleep in the era. There was the capital of Tenno near by. The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.] In 965, {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|qid=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}} ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''kami'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Hirano Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116-117. The shrine has been the site of a cherry blossom festival since 985. The long history of festivals at the Shrine began during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kazan|en|Emperor Kazan|qid=Q361000|lt=Emperor Kazan|1=Emperor Kazan}}, and it has become the oldest regularly held festival in Kyoto. Each year, the festival begins in the morning with a ceremony at the mausoleum of former Emperor Kazan. In the afternoon, a procession travels from the shrine into the neighbor area and back. On 26 August 2018, the shrine suffered extensive damage from [[:en:Typhoon Jebi (2018)|Typhoon Jebi]], which it has struggled to recover from. The haiden was destroyed, as well as a few trees around the shrine. The enshrined ''[[kami]]'' includes: *{{Nihongo||今木神|Imaki-no-kami}} *{{Nihongo||久度神|Kudo-no-kami}} *{{Nihongo||古開神|Furuaki-no-kami}} *{{Nihongo||比売神|[[Himegami|Hime-no-kami]]}} From 1871 through 1946, the Hirano Shrine was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo||官幣大社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} * {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|en|Twenty-Two Shrines|qid=Q215320|lt=Twenty-Two Shrines|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist|1}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/3994492 OCLC 399449] * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== * [http://www.hiranojinja.com/ Official website] {{in lang|ja}} {{coord|35|01|57|N|135|43|55|E|display=title|region:JP-26_type:landmark_source:dewiki}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Kasuga Faith}} [[en:Hirano Shrine]] [[arz:هيرانو جينجا]] {{Commons category|Hirano-jinja}} [[de:Hirano-Schrein]] [[es:Santuario Hirano]] [[fr:Hirano-jinja]] [[ja:平野神社]] [[ru:Хирано-дзиндзя]] [[zh:平野神社]] {{wikidata link|Q738971}} [[Category:8th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga-zukuri]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato no Fuhito clan]] [[Category:Senior First Rank]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior First Rank]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior First Rank]] [[Category:Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Second Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Second Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
